description: Legacy Code Preparation for Migration - non-functional refactorings in code/ that eliminate recurring friction points in the iterative domain migration. Every story must preserve existing Windows/MSBuild behavior.

userStories.title: Replace std::exception("msg") with std::runtime_error("msg") in domain code
userStories.description: As a migration engineer, I want all message-bearing exception throws to use a standard, portable type so that domain code compiles on non-MSVC toolchains.
acceptanceCriteria: No `throw std::exception("...")` remains in domain files in `code/` (verify by grep)
`meosException` (and any similar message-carrying exception class) inherits from `std::runtime_error` instead of `std::exception`
All existing `catch (const std::exception&)` blocks still catch these exceptions (std::runtime_error derives from std::exception)
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes with no new warnings/errors
userStories.notes: meosException already inherited std::runtime_error. Added <stdexcept> to StdAfx.h and replaced all throw std::exception(...) with throw std::runtime_error(...) across 11 files.

userStories.title: Make StringCache and Localizer thread-safe via thread_local
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared per-thread caches to use `thread_local` storage so they are both thread-safe and portable (no `GetCurrentThreadId()` + global map).
acceptanceCriteria: `StringCache::getInstance()` returns a `thread_local static StringCache` instance directly; the `GetCurrentThreadId()` lookup and global map are removed
`translate()` in `localizer.cpp` uses `thread_local` for its rotating-buffer state instead of plain `static`
No `<windows.h>`-only API remains in either implementation for thread identification
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: Both changes were already in place: StringCache::getInstance() uses thread_local static StringCache instance (meos_util.cpp:49), and LocalizerImpl::translate() uses thread_local for int i and wstring value[] (localizer.cpp:145-147). No GetCurrentThreadId() in either. No code changes needed.

userStories.title: Header hygiene: remove StdAfx from .hpp, add explicit includes, fix opaque enum fwd decls
userStories.description: As a non-PCH build, I want each header to be self-contained so it can be consumed without depending on precompiled-header injection or transitive includes.
acceptanceCriteria: `#include "StdAfx.h"` is removed from `.hpp` files (e.g., `intkeymap.hpp`) and from any other standalone header where it appears; required standard headers are added explicitly
Opaque `enum KeyCommandCode;` (and any similar fwd decls) is replaced with an explicit underlying type (e.g., `enum KeyCommandCode : int;`) or the full include
`oTeam.h` includes `<set>` directly
Domain `.cpp` files that use `oDataContainer` include `oDataContainer.h` directly instead of relying on transitive inclusion through `oBase.h`
`oClass.cpp` includes `xmlparser.h` and `timeconstants.hpp` directly
Include-what-you-use pass over domain headers: missing includes (that previously resolved only via PCH) are added
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: Removed #include StdAfx.h from oBase.h, toolbar.h, qualification_final.h. Added <string>/<utility> to oBase.h, <string> to qualification_final.h. Added #include oDataContainer.h directly to oBase.cpp, oCard.cpp, oClub.cpp, oControl.cpp, oCourse.cpp, oTeam.cpp, oPunch.cpp, oRunnerData.cpp, MeosSQL.cpp. Verified all other criteria already satisfied.

userStories.title: Make oData buffers portable across sizeof(wchar_t)
userStories.description: As a Linux build engineer, I want `oData` blob buffers to align correctly for wide-character access and to size themselves based on the platform's `wchar_t` so the same blob layout serializes on both platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: `BYTE oData[dataSize]` declarations in `oClass`, `oClub`, `oCourse`, `oControl`, `oRunner`, and `oTeam` are decorated with `alignas(sizeof(wchar_t))`
`dataSize` constants for these entities are expressed in terms of `sizeof(wchar_t)` (e.g., `N * static_cast<int>(sizeof(wchar_t)) + K`) and evaluate to the same integer value on Windows as today
On Windows the compiled struct layout and `sizeof` for each entity are unchanged
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: All 6 entity headers (oClass, oClub, oCourse, oControl, oRunner, oTeam) already had alignas(sizeof(wchar_t)) on oData/oDataOld and dataSize expressed via sizeof(wchar_t). No code changes needed.

userStories.title: Add public accessors required by migration
userStories.description: As a repository / REST / test author, I want public read access (and where needed, write access) to domain state without using `friend` or layout assumptions. Purely additive — existing call sites unaffected.
acceptanceCriteria: `oPunch::isUsed`, `oPunch::tIndex`, `oPunch::tMatchControlId` are public (or exposed via public accessors)
`oClass` exposes `int getNumLegs() const { return (int)legInfo.size(); }`
`oAbstractRunner::tInTeam`, `tLeg`, `tStartTime` are public (or exposed via public accessors)
`oAbstractRunner` exposes `const TempResult& getTempResult() const`
`oTeam` exposes a public `wstring getRunnerIdString() const` wrapper
`oCard` exposes `void setCardNo(int c)` if not already present
`oBase` (and each entity that owns an `oData` blob) exposes `const BYTE* getOData() const` and `static int getODataBlobSize()`
All additions are purely additive — no existing code is broken
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: Added virtual const BYTE* getOData() const { return nullptr; } to oBase.h. All other criteria were already satisfied: oPunch fields public, oClass::getNumLegs() present, oAbstractRunner::tStartTime public, tLeg/tInTeam public in oRunner, getTempResult() const present, oTeam::getRunnerIdString() present, oCard::setCardNo() present, all entity headers have getOData() and getODataBlobSize().

userStories.title: Replace Win32 CompareString with standard wstring comparison
userStories.description: As a Linux build engineer, I want sort comparisons in domain code to use portable `std::wstring` comparison so domain code does not depend on Win32 NLS APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: `CompareString` calls in `GeneralResultInfo::compareResult` and `oAbstractRunner::compareClubs` are replaced with plain `wstring` comparison (`<` / `compare`)
No `CompareString`/`CompareStringEx` calls remain in domain files in `code/` (verify by grep)
ASCII sort order is preserved (minor differences in non-ASCII collation acceptable)
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: GeneralResultInfo::compareResult and oAbstractRunner::compareClubs already used standard wstring comparison. Replaced the only remaining CompareString call in TabSI.cpp (a sort lambda) with a->getName() < b->getName().

userStories.title: Misc portability: SQL_quote uses toUTF8, oListParam additive members, permute via std::shuffle
userStories.description: As a migration engineer, I want a small set of remaining portability and completeness fixes so the legacy code stops repeatedly tripping the same migration patches.
acceptanceCriteria: `SQL_quote()` uses `toUTF8(wstring)` from `meos_util.h` instead of calling `WideCharToMultiByte` directly
`oListParam` exposes `set<int> selection`, `bool lockUpdate`, and a working `filterInclude()` implementation, with sane defaults so existing call sites are unaffected
`permute()` is implemented using `std::shuffle` with `std::mt19937` seeded from `std::random_device`; any Win32-specific RNG paths are removed
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: All three criteria already in place. SQL_quote (oDataContainer.cpp:929) already calls toUTF8(in) — no WideCharToMultiByte. oListParam already has set<int> selection, bool lockUpdate=false, and filterInclude() implemented in oListInfo.cpp:122. permute() (random.cpp:86-91) already uses std::shuffle with static std::mt19937 seeded from std::random_device. No code changes needed.

userStories.title: Move shared enums (SpecialPunch, RunnerStatus, DynamicRunnerStatus, SortOrder) out of oEvent.h
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to include just an enum without dragging in the entire `oEvent` header so domain headers stay loosely coupled. Re-export from `oEvent.h` for backward compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: `SpecialPunch` is defined in `oControl.h` (or a dedicated enum header) and `oEvent.h` re-exports it (e.g., `using SpecialPunch = ::SpecialPunch;`)
`RunnerStatus`, `DynamicRunnerStatus`, and `SortOrder` are defined in `oAbstractRunner.h` (or a shared enum header) and `oEvent.h` re-exports them
All existing code that includes `oEvent.h` still compiles unchanged
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: All criteria already satisfied. RunnerStatus, DynamicRunnerStatus, SortOrder are defined at global scope in oAbstractRunner.h. SpecialPunch is in oPunch class with global alias in oControl.h (using SpecialPunch = oPunch::SpecialPunch). oEvent.h includes oRunner.h→oAbstractRunner.h and oControl.h directly, so all enums are re-exported transitively. No code changes needed.

userStories.title: Extract oAbstractRunner to its own header to break oRunner/oEvent circular include
userStories.description: As a migration engineer, I want the `oRunner` / `oEvent` circular include resolved at the source so each migration run doesn't have to re-do the same extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: New header `oAbstractRunner.h` contains the full `oAbstractRunner` definition and only forward-declares `oEvent`
`oRunner.h` and `oTeam.h` include `oAbstractRunner.h` and no longer define `oAbstractRunner` themselves
`oEvent.h` no longer depends on the full definition of `oRunner` to use `oAbstractRunner` pointers/references
Methods that need the full `oEvent` definition (e.g., `DynamicValue` methods touching `oEvent::dataRevision`) live in `.cpp` files, not in the new header
Legacy MSBuild build of `code/` passes
userStories.notes: All acceptance criteria were already satisfied from a prior run (commit US-027). oAbstractRunner.h exists with the full oAbstractRunner class definition and only uses oEvent via the forward declaration in oBase.h (class oEvent; at line 35). oRunner.h includes oAbstractRunner.h (no oEvent.h in its include closure). oTeam.h includes oAbstractRunner.h directly. oEvent.h includes oRunner.h for vector<oRunner> storage, but does not require it for oAbstractRunner pointers. DynamicValue::isOld/update/get/reset are implemented in oRunner.cpp (not inline in the header). No circular include remains: oRunner.h → oAbstractRunner.h chain has no path back to oEvent.h. No code changes needed.
